title: Life Biosciences Lands $80M Series D for ER-100 Partial Reprogramming Trial
description: Life Biosciences closed $80M Series D to fund Phase 1 of ER-100, its partial epigenetic 'cell reset' therapy targeting glaucoma and optic neuropathies. The raise crystallizes ER-100 as the first clinical-stage partial-reprogramming asset in humans, using retinal ganglion cell rejuvenation as a measurable beachhead before systemic aging-reversal applications.

title: Friday Biotech Rout: XBI -3.08%, SBIO -3.12%, ARKG -3.53%
description: Friday delivered a sector-wide biotech selloff with XBI -3.08%, SBIO -3.12%, ARKG -3.53%, IBB -2.63%, BBH -2.27%, and IDNA -2.47% all crushed alongside XLV -1.04%. Even med-devices (IHI -0.62%) and aging-population (AGNG -1.29%) participated — pure risk-off into the weekend, undoing Tuesday's rally and then some, with no longevity-specific catalyst beyond macro repositioning.

title: GeneRare Raises EUR 20M Series A for Microbial Genome Drug Discovery
description: GeneRare closed a EUR 20M ($21.6M) Series A to scale microbial genome scanning for novel natural-product molecules, having already identified 200+ hits in 2025. The fund expands libraries from plants, fungi, and marine sources with explicit ambitions toward geroprotectors including next-generation rapamycin analogs — adding AI-discovered natural products as a fourth pillar alongside small molecules, gene therapy, and cell therapy.

title: Vitalist Bay 2026 Day 2 Programming Anchored by AI x Bio and Replacement
description: Vitalist Bay's second day at Berkeley's Lighthaven leaned heavily into AI x Bio and Replacement tracks, with startup pitch sessions accelerating capital formation in the morning slot. With 100+ speakers including Aubrey de Grey, Joe Betts-LaCroix, Michael Snyder and Morgan Levine across four days, the festival is now widely viewed as the field's annual private-market price discovery event.

title: Noom Acquires 503A Compounding Pharmacy to Bundle GLP-1s with Longevity Coaching
description: Noom's vertical-integration play through acquisition of a 503A compounding pharmacy lets it dispense GLP-1s, peptides like sermorelin, and NAD+ alongside behavioral coaching for healthy aging. The move pairs app data with pharmacy access at scale — and validates the field's emerging GLP-1-as-first-longevity-drug thesis at the consumer go-to-market layer rather than purely in pharma R&D.

title: Longevity Biotech Stocks Rally as $27T Healthcare Transformation Accelerates
description: Sector-wide rally narrative builds around cell-restoration therapies as catalysts for a $27 trillion global healthcare transformation, though specific stock percentages were not disclosed.

title: Longevity News Roundup: Life Biosciences $80M, GeneRare $21.6M, Noom Pharmacy
description: Phil Newman and Nina Patrick cover Life Biosciences $80M Series D for ER-100 partial reprogramming Phase 1, GeneRare EUR 20M Series A, Noom 503A pharmacy buy, Annovis buntanetap Phase 3, Agentis-Ultrahuman wearables, and Peloton-Longevity Show partnership.

title: Avai Bio Introduces Klotho-Based Anti-Aging Therapy at Premier Forum
description: Avai Bio (OTCQB: AVAI) details its cell-based alpha-Klotho longevity therapy via Klothonova JV with Austrianova; data to be presented at Second Annual Klotho Conference in September.

title: Dietary Changes in Older People Can Improve Biological Age
description: Aging Cell paper from Dr Caitlin Andrews shows 65-75 year olds who reduced fat or animal-based protein for four weeks registered measurable reductions in biological age via biomarker profile.

title: Scientists Reversed Biological Age in Older Adults With 4-Week Diet Change
description: Coverage of the Sydney Aging Cell study highlights strongest reductions in the omnivorous higher-carb / lower-fat arm, with closest-to-baseline group showing no change.

title: Insilico Announces Industry's First Longevity Board
description: Andrew Adams (Lilly) chairs new Longevity Board with Nobel laureate Michael Levitt; mandate covers life models, biomarkers of aging, dual-purpose targets, and clinical validation of healthspan effects.

title: Human Longevity Launches $599 Clinical-Grade Whole Genome Sequencing
description: Human Longevity, Inc. unveiled a $599 clinical-grade whole genome report with AI-driven prevention insights, honoring founder J. Craig Venter's legacy.

title: Large Rapamycin Clinical Trial Launches at UT Health San Antonio
description: Multi-phase clinical study with ~84 older adults will determine optimal rapamycin dosage for healthy aging via a six-month treatment, six-month follow-up RCT against placebo and intermittent dosing.

title: Annovis Advances Buntanetap in Phase 3 Alzheimer's and Parkinson's Trials
description: Annovis's buntanetap, derived from the Calabar bean, reduces amyloid beta, tau, and alpha-synuclein translation in Phase 3 Alzheimer's and Parkinson's; sildenafil combos boost cerebral blood flow via NO-cGMP pathway.

title: Vitalist Bay 2026 Set to Spotlight Longevity's Next Phase
description: Four-day Vitalist Bay festival at Lighthaven, Berkeley brings together researchers, founders, clinicians and investors across biotechnology, AI x Bio, biostasis, and health optimization.

title: BioAge Labs R&D Day: BGE-102 Phase 1 Shows Big hsCRP Drops
description: BioAge presented Phase 1 results for BGE-102 with 24-hour coverage at 60 mg once-daily dosing and profound hsCRP reductions in obese subjects, setting up cardiovascular and DME POC studies.

title: Creatine Shows Synergy With Exercise in Older Adults
description: Spanish researchers in Experimental Gerontology showed creatine combined with power training improved BDNF, antioxidant defense, inflammation, and lower-limb function in 103 older adults over 16 weeks.

title: Longevity News Roundup — Week 19, 2026
description: Roundup examines multi-target Alzheimer's therapy, sugar glycation in aging, personalized CRISPR for Parkinson's, FDA real-time trials, and peptide platforms.

title: ARDD 12th Meeting Report — Toward Actionable Interventions in Human Aging
description: Aging-US Volume 18 publishes the ARDD synthesis: epigenome as central regulator, partial cellular reprogramming, and systemic effects pointing to organ-wide rejuvenation.

title: New Senolytic Strategy Targets GPX4 in Ageing Cells
description: Gil and Tate groups identify chloroacetamide senolytics that selectively kill senescent cells by inhibiting GPX4 and triggering ferroptosis.

title: Rubedo Reports Positive Phase 1 Results for RLS-1496
description: First-in-class topical GPX4 modulator was well tolerated with dose-related target engagement, senescent-cell reduction, lower inflammatory cytokines, and ~20% reduction in epidermal thickness.

title: Insilico Medicine and Eli Lilly Sign $2.75B AI Drug Discovery Pact
description: Lilly grants Insilico $115M upfront with up to $2.75B in milestones plus tiered royalties for exclusive worldwide license to a pipeline of oral preclinical assets.

title: Agentis Partners Ultrahuman to Integrate CGM/HRV into Longevity Quotient
description: Wearable partnership embeds continuous glucose and HRV data into Agentis' Longevity Quotient platform; separately Well Health-AliveCor AI-ECG AFib detection rolls out in Canada.

title: Want to Reverse Your Biological Age? Change What You Eat
description: Inc. covers the Sydney study, noting older adults reducing animal-derived proteins and fats saw notable improvements in cholesterol, insulin, and blood pressure within four weeks.

title: Q1 2026 Longevity Biotech Investment Hits $3.74B Across 49 Deals
description: DLT dataset projects 2026 capital flows of $8-9B (55-60% YoY growth), with median deal size of $21.8M signaling a maturing pipeline.

title: Inexion: $554M Q4 Longevity Funding Yet RWE Data Gap Persists
description: Q4 2025 longevity biotech funding hit $554M (900% above Q3), but no major claims-first data platform reaches the cash-pay longevity cohort.

title: Seragon SRN-901 Extends Median Lifespan 33% in Aged Mice
description: SRN-901 multipathway treatment of 18-month-old mice produced a 33% increase in median remaining lifespan plus reductions in frailty and tumor incidence.

title: NYT Magazine: Cellular Rejuvenation Has the Potential to Reverse Aging
description: Long-form feature on partial reprogramming, Altos Labs, and the first safety trial of rejuvenation therapy in humans targeting glaucoma.

title: Junyue Cao on How the Body Ages, Cell by Cell
description: Rockefeller researcher discusses ultra-high-throughput single-cell technologies applied to mapping cellular hallmarks of aging across tissues.

title: Sleep Apnea, Intermittent Hypoxia, and p16-Mediated Senescence
description: Preprint links sleep apnea-induced intermittent hypoxia to p16Ink4a senescence in vascular endothelium, suggesting senescence component to CPAP-resistant cardiovascular risk.
